heading: Repealed. , title III, § 301(a)(1), (3), (5),July 28, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712
label: 46b to 48a Repealed. , title III, § 301(a)(1), (3), (5),July 28, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712
notes: Section 46b, , , established detention hospitals at Fairbanks and Nome for temporary care and detention of insane.act June 25, 1910, ch. 424, § 136 Stat. 852

Section 46c, acts , ; , ch, 772, title III, § 301(b)(1), , defined terms used in sections 46, 47a to 47c, 48a, 50, and 50a of this title.Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 156 Stat. 78270 Stat. 712July 28, 1956

Section 47, , , related to commitment of insane in Alaska, provided for compensation of commissioners, jurors, and witnesses, and prescribed method of payment of compensation, mileage, fees, and other expenses.act Jan. 27, 1905, ch. 277, § 833 Stat. 619
Section 47a, , , related to custody, use, and return of money and personal property of committed persons.act Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 356 Stat. 783

Section 47b, acts , ; , title III, § 301(b)(1), , related to discharge of patients from mental institutions, permitted leaves of absences to patients, and required issuance of suitable clothing upon discharge.Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 656 Stat. 783July 28, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712

Section 47c, acts , ; , title III, § 301(b)(1), , authorized superintendent of any mental institution to board patients with private families, provided for inspection, and empowered superintendent to remove patients from boarding places.Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 756 Stat. 784July 28, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712

Section 48, acts , ; , title III, § 301(b)(1), , provided for a statement of legal residence of insane persons in commitment papers, required return of nonresident patients, and for payment of expenses in connection with such return.Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 956 Stat. 785July 28, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712

Section 48a, acts , ; , title III, § 301(b)(1), , required payment of expenses of care by patient, his legal representative, spouse, parents, or adult children.Oct. 14, 1942, ch. 601, § 956 Stat. 785July 21, 1956, ch. 77270 Stat. 712
